title: Tomark II

Furthermore, {{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-sectors-of-the-mid-rim|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Sectors of the Mid Rim}} depicts the Ividal [[Sector/Legends|sector]] as being located between the [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroutes]] ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' identifies as the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]] and the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]]. Finally, ''The Essential Atlas'' also designates the portion of the Mid Rim situated between those two [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] as being a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]].</ref>

{{Quote|Only when you are safely en route should you play this holovid. It contains information on your mission. I wish I could tell you more, but I fear there are traitors at the base.|Captain Amarith, to a group of Rebel agents|<ref name="Journal11" />}}
'''Tomark II''', also known as '''Tomark''', was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Ividal sector/Legends|Ividal sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it was the site of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] base known as [[Chiron Base]], from which attacks on [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] were carried out.

By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Empire considered Tomark one of its priority targets for its [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. At some point during the conflict, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Amarith]] dispatched a team of Rebel agents on a [[Mission to Ando|mission]] that took them from Tomark II to the planet [[Ando/Legends|Ando]].

==Description==
Tomark II,<ref name="Journal11" /> also referred to simply as Tomark,<ref name="IH">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref> was a terrestrial<ref name="Journal11" /> [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="IH" /> that occupied the second [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="Journal11" /> in the [[Tomark system/Legends|Tomark system]], a part of the [[Ividal sector/Legends|Ividal sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion<ref name="Slice" /> of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated at a distance of several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours']] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel from the planet [[Ando/Legends|Ando]],<ref name="Journal11" /> located in the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]].<ref name="EAOC Ando">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Ando system/Legends|Ando system]]</ref>

==History==
===A Rebel base===
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Tomark II was the site of [[Chiron Base]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] facility housing a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] used for hit-and-run attacks on [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]]. The world's [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] location made it ideal for staging such operations.<ref name="Journal11" />

The Empire eventually came to suspect Tomark of housing an [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance Military]] facility<ref name="IH" /> and, by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' is presented as an [[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide|in-universe document]] which includes the report "[[IMQ-54544-XX-07 Security Clearance Black DS-1 Orbital Battle Station]]" and was published nineteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which corresponds to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> it and several other worlds with unconfirmed potential Rebel presence were listed as priority targets for the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] in an [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] report entitled "[[IMQ-54544-XX-07 Security Clearance Black DS-1 Orbital Battle Station]]."<ref name="IH" />

===Departing on a mission===
{{Quote|Thank you for responding to my urgent summons. I apologize for disturbing your sleep, but a delicate situation has arisen. You are ordered to proceed directly to the ''Gambit'', my personal ship, without discussing this meeting with anyone. Board, take off, and plan a hyperspace jump using the coordinates in the nav computer to proceed to your destination.|Captain Amarith, to a group of Rebel agents|<ref name="Journal11" />}}
At some point during the Galactic Civil War, the Empire initiated a secret operation known as the [[Ando Project]]. Unaware of the operation but nevertheless worried by the unusual increase in Imperial activity on Ando, the retired [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] Rebel operative [[Koral-tae]] contacted the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Amarith]] of Chiron Base for help. In response, Amarith summoned a team of the Tomark II facility's resident agents for a mission briefing.<ref name="Journal11" />

However, since the captain suspected the presence of traitors at the base, the only verbal instructions she gave to the team were to use her personal [[Luxury 2800|space yacht]], the ''[[Gambit]]'', to leave Tomark II using the hyperspace coordinates present in the [[Yacht/Legends|yacht's]] [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]]. In addition, Amarith provided the Rebel agents with a [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]], which contained information on their [[Mission to Ando|mission]] and which the team was only to view when its [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was already en route to its destination. Subsequently, the Rebel agents boarded the ''Gambit'' and departed Chiron Base, leaving Tomark II for Ando, the destination of their mission.<ref name="Journal11" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:StarWarsAdventureJournal11.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tomark II was introduced in ''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11.]]
Tomark II first appeared in "[[The Ando Project]]," a [[roleplaying game]] adventure authored by [[Chris Doyle]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|eleventh issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' in [[November]] [[1996]]. The illustrations in the adventure, done by [[Matt Busch]], depict a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] as one of Chiron Base's Rebel agents dispatched from Tomark II. However, this article does not treat that as a [[canon]]ical fact with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Journal11" />

In "The Ando Project," the player characters follow the trail of the Ando Project from Ando to an [[Aquaculture Facility|Imperial facility]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Uridia]]. If, after investigating—and possibly sabotaging or even destroying—the facility, the characters manage to escape it and leave the [[Uridia system/Legends|Uridia system]], they return to Chiron Base on Tomark II, where they submit their mission report to Captain Amarith and may be commended, depending on their actions on Uridia.<ref name="Journal11" />

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Tomark [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Tomark II, in grid square Q-14.<ref name="TEA" /> On a map included in the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] astronomical object [[Thomork]] was mislabeled as "Tomark,"<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> a proper noun which the [[2014]] ''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' also introduced as a shortened designation for Tomark II.<ref name="IH" />
